title: ctxd Event
description:
A CloudEvents v1.0 event with ctxd extensions. This schema is the source of truth for all SDK code generation (Rust, Python, TS/JS) and MUST stay in sync with `crates/ctxd-core/src/event.rs`.

Notes on canonical form:
 - For hashing and signing, the canonical form is JSON with keys sorted alphabetically (BTreeMap order), the `predecessorhash` and `signature` fields removed, `parents` always present as a lexicographically-sorted array of UUID strings (empty array when none), and `attestation` always present as either a hex-encoded string or JSON `null`.
 - On the *wire*, optional fields (`predecessorhash`, `signature`, `parents` when empty, `attestation` when None) are omitted to keep payloads small. Consumers MUST treat absent optional fields as defaults.

specversion.description: CloudEvents spec version. Always `1.0` for v0.3 ctxd.

id.description: Globally-unique event id. UUIDv7 so the natural ordering is monotonically time-aligned.

source.description: Identifies the context in which the event happened. For ctxd this is typically the daemon URI (e.g. `ctxd://my-host`).

subject.description: Subject path the event is filed under. Must start with `/` and contain non-empty path segments.

type.description: Event type discriminator (e.g. `ctx.note`, `ctx.document`, `demo`). On the Rust side this maps to the `event_type` field; CloudEvents requires the wire key `type`.

time.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when the event was created.

datacontenttype.description: Content type of `data`. Defaults to `application/json` when omitted.

data.description: Event payload. JSON of any shape. SDKs MAY decode it into a stronger type if `datacontenttype` is `application/json` and the consumer knows the schema.

predecessorhash.description: SHA-256 hash of the predecessor event's canonical form, scoped per subject tree. Hex-encoded, lowercase, exactly 64 chars. Absent on the first event in a chain.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical form. Hex-encoded, lowercase, exactly 128 chars. Absent for unsigned events.

parents.description: Parent event ids, used to represent concurrent / branch merges in the federated log. When omitted on the wire, treat as empty. The canonical form always includes this field as a lexicographically-sorted array.

attestation.description: Optional TEE attestation blob. Hex-encoded bytes on the wire. ctxd does not interpret this field — it is carried end-to-end through federation. The canonical form encodes this as a hex string when present, JSON `null` when absent.
